_comment: Grafana dashboard panels for the HTTP /api/login migration deprecation telemetry.
Drop these into an existing dashboard via the 'Import JSON' panel option, or
merge into a fresh dashboard JSON under .panels[]. Counters are emitted by
1. Stacked-area: legacy vs new request rate over time.
ARCH §12 T+1 exit criterion = legacy line trends to (and stays near) zero.
2. Single-stat: legacy / total ratio for the last 5 minutes.
Per-auth_type breakdowns are available by adding `auth_type` to the legend
(e.g. legend: '{{path}} / {{auth_type}}'); useful when WeChat/QQ stay on
legacy longer than password as third-party SDKs roll out.

panels.title: Login Path Request Rate (legacy vs new)
panels.description: rate of ClientPlayerLogin.Login calls, split by transport. ARCH §12 T+1 exit criterion: legacy must trend to zero before flipping LegacyGateLoginEnabled=false.

panels.title: Legacy Path Share (last 5m)
panels.description: Fraction of recent logins still on the legacy gate-TCP path. >5% = block T+2; <1% = T+2 ready.

panels.title: Login Path by Auth Type
panels.description: Per-auth_type breakdown to spot 'wechat traffic stuck on legacy' vs 'password fully migrated' patterns.

_comment: Alertmanager rule (paste under groups.rules in alert-rules.yaml).
Fires when legacy traffic stays >5% for 10 minutes — that's the
rollback signal, not the T+2 trigger. Pair with a separate one-shot
alert when legacy drops to zero for 24h to ping ops about T+2 readiness.

annotations.summary: Legacy gate-Login path receiving >5% of login traffic
annotations.description: ARCH §12 expected this to drop after T+1 cutover. Investigate before flipping LegacyGateLoginEnabled=false.
